title: The Top 10 Movies Scenes of 2023 and ‘American Fiction’ With Cord Jefferson
format: The last recording of the year. Sean and Amanda count down their favorite movie scenes of 2023, five each and alternating, then review 'American Fiction' before Sean sits down with its writer-director Cord Jefferson.

films.title: Past Lives
films.note: No. 1 - the closing Uber ride. Sean calls it the best ending of the year, a simple, fully intentional use of cinematic language he'd talked through with Celine Song on the show.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: No. 2 - the epilogue near the end, and who delivers it. A bold artistic choice from their collective No. 1 movie of the year; Sean keeps it vague to avoid the spoiler.

films.title: Anatomy of a Fall
films.note: No. 3 - the recorded argument between the couple, played back in court and dramatized between the two actors. It changes how you read the whole film.

films.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
films.note: No. 4 - a cheat, two scenes: the overhead Dragon's Breath shootout borrowed from first-person shooters, and the Sacre-Coeur stairs fight. Amanda took the stairs; Sean likes the flamethrower.

films.title: Beau Is Afraid
films.note: No. 5 - the Mariah Carey sex scene, the hardest Sean laughed in a theater all year. Shout-out to Parker Posey and the prosthetics work.

films.title: The Taste of Things
films.note: No. 1 - the opening feast and its preparation. Shot like an action sequence, the Mission: Impossible of food; balletic, in media res, treating food as both art and love. Wider U.S. release Feb. 9.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 2 - the Trinity test. Amanda rented it and skipped to the scene; the cut to silence makes it the best in-theater moment of the year. Sean was amused she picked it first.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
films.note: No. 3 - the Rome car chase, screwball action shot on location. The train sequence and the Venice bridge fight were runners-up in the same movie.

films.title: Maestro
films.note: No. 4 - Bradley Cooper conducting Mahler in the cathedral, six minutes of music, sweating, and prosthetics. 'Total cinema.'

films.title: Leave the World Behind
films.note: No. 5 - the pile-up of driverless white Teslas, an image about tech and idiocy. It had topped Netflix's chart for about 15 days at recording.

segments.heading: 'American Fiction'
segments.note: Sean and Amanda on the movie before the interview

films.title: American Fiction
films.note: Cord Jefferson's debut, adapted from Percival Everett's 2001 novel 'Erasure.' Jeffrey Wright as Monk, a frustrated writer who dashes off a parody of 'black' fiction that sells for a fortune. Half biting publishing satire, half quiet family drama; the pair split on which half lands harder. A murderers' row cast (Sterling K. Brown, Tracee Ellis Ross, Issa Rae, Erika Alexander, Leslie Uggams) and a chilling Adam Brody cameo. They peg it for a Best Picture nomination and Wright for Best Actor.

films.title: Hollywood Shuffle
films.note: Robert Townsend's satire, a touchstone Jefferson keeps citing; he notes it has little of the family-drama half.

films.title: Bamboozled
films.note: Spike Lee's media satire, the other comparison point critics reach for.

films.title: Friends with Money
films.note: Jefferson's favorite Nicole Holofcener film, and a model for pairing a comedy of manners with intimate character study.

films.title: The Best Man Holiday
films.note: Sean's example of the upper-middle-class black family story told before, usually as rom-com rather than something this probing.

films.title: Sound of Freedom
films.note: Jefferson's shorthand for the polarized, everyone-in-their-corner media landscape he made a deliberately big-tent movie against.

films.title: Carnal Knowledge
films.note: His last-great-thing pick, Mike Nichols with Jack Nicholson and Art Garfunkel; he saw it for the first time recently and it blew his socks off.

Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Par.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
